content: Q1: Daily Reflection writes — plan proposes always creating memory/reflection-YYYY-MM-DD.md files instead of appending to MEMORY.md, keeping MEMORY.md user-curated. Agree?
Q2: Integration API HTTP framework — plan uses Node.js built-in http module (zero new deps) instead of Express. Project has no HTTP server deps currently. Start minimal, add Express later if needed?

Q3: Gmail access scope — Phase 3 uses gmail.readonly. Draft sending (Phase 4) would need gmail.send scope added later. Approved drafts initially just move to sent/ folder without actual sending. Agree?

Q4: Habits auto-detection — should detection rules be limited to predefined safe commands (git, wc) or allow arbitrary user-configured shell commands? Arbitrary adds flexibility but expands attack surface.

Q5: Integration API process isolation — for hard credential isolation, integ-api should ALWAYS run as separate child process from daemon (not in-process). Daemon spawns it, never loads credentials directly. Agree?

Project: TypeScript ESM personal assistant (Node 22+, Claude Agent SDK, vitest, pino, zod). 14 implementation tasks across 4 phases. Key architecture: factory functions with DI, co-located tests (foo.test.ts), Zod-validated config in src/core/types.ts, daemon.ts orchestrates all subsystems. Build: npm run build (tsc + template copy). Test: npx vitest run. No linter. Config loaded from ~/.personal-assistant/settings.json. Entry points: src/cli.ts, src/daemon.ts, src/terminal.ts. First ready task: ac-51c0a369 (Config schema extensions) — all other tasks depend on it. After config, these can parallelize: daily-log-indexing, pre-compaction-flush, heartbeat-state-diffing, git-sync, integ-api-server. Key files to modify: src/core/types.ts (Zod schemas), src/core/config.ts (defaults), src/daemon.ts (subsystem init), src/heartbeat/scheduler.ts + prompts.ts, src/memory/collect-files.ts + indexer.ts, src/session/compactor.ts. Follow existing patterns: factory functions, pino logger, Zod validation. All new files go in src/ matching existing dir structure. Use Anthropic API directly (fetch) for LLM calls like compactor.ts does.
